title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 1391
content: Anemia in chronic kidney disease is classified as an “Other deficiency anemia,” placing it within a broader category of deficiency‑related blood disorders. The “Other deficiency anemias” group includes folate‑deficiency anemia (فقر الدم الناتج عن نقص الفولات), iron‑deficiency anemia (فقر الدم حديدي الأرومات), and a non‑regenerative form of anemia (فقر الدم اللا تنسجي) as a subcategory. It also encompasses hemolytic anemia that is not caused by autoimmune mechanisms (فقر دم انحلالي ليس ناجمًا عن المناعة الذاتية). By situating anemia in chronic kidney disease among these specific deficiency and hemolytic conditions, clinicians can appreciate the diverse etiologic pathways—nutrient deficiencies and non‑autoimmune hemolysis—that may contribute to anemia in this patient population, guiding more comprehensive diagnostic evaluation and targeted therapeutic strategies.

title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 2 -- Community 713
content: The category “اضطرابات مناعية/المناعة الذاتية (SMQ)” encompasses a range of hematologic and inflammatory conditions, including فقر الدم اللا تنسجي (aplastic anemia), فقر الدم الانحلالي المكتسب (acquired hemolytic anemia), فقر دم انحلالي ليس ناجمًا عن المناعة الذاتية (hemolytic anemia not caused by autoimmunity), التهاب المفاصل (arthritis), and التهاب الشريان التاجي (coronary artery inflammation). These entities are grouped together because they involve immune system dysregulation, whether through autoantibody‑mediated attacks, cell‑mediated immune injury, or broader immune‑related inflammatory processes. While most of the listed disorders are driven by autoimmune mechanisms, the non‑autoimmune hemolytic anemia is included in the classification for organizational purposes despite its different pathogenesis. Recognizing that these diverse diseases share an underlying immune component helps clinicians identify common diagnostic pathways and consider immunomodulatory therapies across different organ systems, thereby improving patient assessment and management.

The group Other deficiency anemias encompasses several specific anemia types, including فقر الدم الناتج عن نقص الفولات (folate‑deficiency anemia), فقر الدم حديدي الأرومات (iron‑deficiency anemia), فقر الدم اللا تنسجي (non‑megaloblastic anemia), فقر الدم الانحلالي المكتسب (acquired hemolytic anemia), and فقر الدم الانحلالي الناجم عن المناعة الذاتية (autoimmune hemolytic anemia). These specific entities are considered children or sub‑classifications of Other deficiency anemias, illustrating how anemia in chronic kidney disease relates to a spectrum of deficiency‑related and hemolytic anemias and informing clinicians’ diagnostic and therapeutic approaches.

title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 3 -- Community 1384
content: Anemia in chronic kidney disease is classified as a form of “Other deficiency anemias.” The category of Other deficiency anemias encompasses several distinct types, including فقر الدم الناتج عن نقص الفولات (folate‑deficiency anemia), فقر الدم حديدي الأرومات (iron‑deficiency anemia), فقر الدم الانحلالي الناجم عن المناعة الذاتية (autoimmune hemolytic anemia), and فقر دم انحلالي ليس ناجمًا عن المناعة الذاتية (non‑immune hemolytic anemia). Recognizing that anemia associated with chronic kidney disease falls within this broader group helps clinicians consider the underlying nutritional or hemolytic mechanisms that may contribute to the patient’s low hemoglobin and guides appropriate diagnostic evaluation and targeted treatment strategies.

title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2661
content: Other complications of pregnancy represent a broad category of maternal health issues that include specific conditions such as الاعتماد على المخدرات، قبل الولادة (drug dependence before birth), خلل وظيفة الدرقية في فترة الحمل (thyroid dysfunction during pregnancy), and فقر الدم في أثناء الحمل (anemia during pregnancy). Recognizing these sub‑conditions under the umbrella of other complications of pregnancy is important for comprehensive prenatal assessment, as each can affect fetal development and maternal outcomes. Effective screening and management of drug dependence, thyroid abnormalities, and anemia are essential components of obstetric care to reduce risks to both mother and child.

title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 2 -- Community 954
content: Anemia in chronic kidney disease is classified as belonging to the broader category of Other deficiency anemias. The category Other deficiency anemias encompasses several specific deficiency disorders, including فقر الدم الناتج عن نقص الفولات (folate‑deficiency anemia), فقر الدم الوبيل (a form of vitamin‑B12‑related anemia), and فقر الدم حديدي الأرومات (iron‑deficiency anemia). In addition, Other deficiency anemias has a child condition called فقر الدم اللا تنسجي (non‑megaloblastic anemia). Recognizing that anemia in chronic kidney disease falls within this group of deficiency‑related anemias guides clinicians to assess for underlying nutrient deficiencies and related subtypes when diagnosing and treating affected patients.

title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 166
content: The community classifies anemia associated with chronic kidney disease under the broader category of other deficiency anemias, which also comprises folate‑deficiency anemia (فقر الدم الناتج عن نقص الفولات, also called فقر الدم الوبيل), iron‑deficiency anemia (فقر الدم حديدي الأرومات), non‑regenerative (aplastic) anemia (فقر الدم اللا تنسجي), acquired hemolytic anemia (فقر الدم الانحلالي المكتسب), autoimmune hemolytic anemia (فقر الدم الانحلالي الناجم عن المناعة الذاتية), non‑autoimmune hemolytic anemia (فقر دم انحلالي ليس ناجمًا عن المناعة الذاتية), and cold‑type hemolytic anemia (فقر الدم الانحلالي من النوع البارد). Thalassemia (الثلاسيميا) is also placed within this group despite being a hemoglobinopathy, and it shares the ocular manifestation of sickle‑cell‑related retinopathy (اعتلال الشبكية بالخلايا المنجلية). title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 2327
content: The category of autoimmune disorders (اضطرابات مناعية/المناعة الذاتية (SMQ)) includes a range of hematologic and inflammatory conditions. Aplastic anemia (فقر الدم الوبيل) is classified as an autoimmune disease, as is non‑regenerative anemia (فقر الدم اللا تنسجي) and acquired hemolytic anemia (فقر الدم الانحلالي المكتسب). Inflammatory joint disease (التهاب المفاصل) and coronary artery inflammation (التهاب الشريان التاجي) are also considered members of this autoimmune group. Recognizing that these diverse diseases share an underlying autoimmune mechanism guides clinicians toward appropriate immunomodulatory treatments and vigilant monitoring for systemic involvement.

title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 3 -- Community 1538
content: Other deficiency anemias is a broad diagnostic category that includes several specific types of anemia. It is classified as فقر الدم الناتج عن نقص الفولات (folate‑deficiency anemia) and as فقر الدم حديدي الأرومات (iron‑deficiency anemia), reflecting its inclusion of nutrient‑deficiency–related blood disorders. In addition, the category is classified as فقر دم انحلالي ليس ناجمًا عن المناعة الذاتية (non‑immune‑mediated hemolytic anemia), indicating that it also encompasses hemolytic anemias that are not caused by autoimmunity. Within this grouping, فقر الدم الانحلالي المكتسب (acquired hemolytic anemia) is identified as a child or subcategory, further specifying a type of hemolysis that develops secondary to external factors. Together, these relationships show that “Other deficiency anemias” serves as an umbrella term that integrates both deficiency‑based anemias (folate and iron) and certain non‑immune hemolytic anemias, including acquired forms, which is important for accurate diagnosis, classification, and patient management.
